title: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
subhead: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

title: Bombing of civilian infrastructure aims to bring Iran to its knees
subhead: The US-Israeli strikes on Iran, which have damaged transportation, industrial and oil infrastructure as well as its military facilities, are designed to deal a blow to the country's regional power, without seeking the complete collapse of the regime.

title: Hungarian PM Orban campaigns on fears of war and hatred of Ukraine
subhead: Threatened in the polls for the April parliamentary elections by Peter Magyar's party, nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban has portrayed his rival as an agent who serves Kyiv and the European Union.

title: PSG treads carefully after ultras display banner supporting star player facing rape trial
subhead: PSG has distanced itself from the banner displayed at the club's Parc des Princes stadium on February 25, while reiterating the 'presumption of innocence' for Achraf Hakimi, ordered to stand trial for rape.

title: Culling of foxes, crows and ravens is 'inefficient, economically unjustifiable and ethically questionable'
subhead: Researchers from the National Museum of Natural History examined the effects of systematically hunting 'species likely to cause damage.' They found that the cost of destroying these animals exceeded the agricultural damage they cause, without reducing that damage.

The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhead: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

title: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
subhead: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.

title: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
subhead: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.
